title: hieracium murorum subsp. subreniforme
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. subreniforme, also known as Hawkweed or Hieracium, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Hieracium murorum subsp. subreniforme can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, but proper identification and dosing are crucial due to potential interactions and side effects.
